assertions.claim_text: The war in Europe concluded with the liberation of German-occupied territories and the invasion of Germany by the Allies which culminated in the fall of Berlin to Soviet troops, and Germany's unconditional surrender on 8 May 1945.
assertions.subject: The war

assertions.claim_text:
after the German Luftwaffe Blitz, December 30, 1940]]

The air Battle of Britain began in early July with Luftwaffe attacks on shipping and harbours.
assertions.subject: The air Battle

assertions.claim_text: Japan conquered much of coastal China and Southeast Asia, but its advances in the Pacific were halted in June 1942 at the Battle of Midway.
assertions.subject: Japan

assertions.claim_text: World War II transformed the political, economic, and social structures of the world, and established the foundation of international relations for the rest of the 20th century and into the 21st century.
assertions.subject: World War II

assertions.claim_text: After the fall of France in June 1940, the war continued mainly between Germany, now assisted by Fascist Italy, and the British Empire/British Commonwealth, with fighting in the Balkans, Mediterranean, and Middle East, East Africa, the aerial Battle of Britain and the Blitz, and the naval Battle of the Atlantic.
assertions.subject: the war

assertions.claim_text: In June 1941, Germany invaded the Soviet Union, opening the Eastern Front.
assertions.subject: Germany

assertions.claim_text: However, other senior German officials like Ribbentrop saw an opportunity to create a Euro-Asian bloc against the British Empire by inviting the Soviet Union into the Tripartite Pact.
assertions.subject: other senior German officials
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: In mid-January 1945, the Red Army attacked in Poland, pushing from the Vistula to the Oder river in Germany, and overran East Prussia.
On 31 July 1940, Hitler decided that the Soviet Union should be eliminated and aimed for the conquest of Ukraine, the Baltic states and Byelorussia.
assertions.subject: Hitler
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: The United Nations was created to foster international cooperation and prevent future conflicts, with the victorious great powers—China, France, the Soviet Union, the UK, and the US—becoming the permanent members of its security council.
assertions.subject: The United Nations

assertions.claim_text: Soviet troops stormed and captured Berlin in late April.
assertions.subject: Soviet troops

assertions.claim_text:
prepares to shoot a mother clutching her child behind the frontline, Ivangorod, Ukraine]]

Although the Red Army was preparing for strategic counter-offensives before the war, Operation Barbarossa forced the Soviet supreme command to adopt strategic defence.
assertions.subject: Operation Barbarossa
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: Japan announced its unconditional surrender on 15 August, and signed a surrender document on 2 September 1945.
assertions.subject: Japan

assertions.claim_text: Major changes in leadership occurred on both sides during this period.
assertions.subject: Major changes

assertions.claim_text: However, their paths soon diverged.
assertions.subject: their paths

assertions.claim_text: In Germany, the western and eastern occupation zones officially ended in 1949, with the respective zones becoming separate countries, West Germany and East Germany..
assertions.subject: the western and eastern occupation zones

assertions.claim_text: Dates for the beginning of the Pacific War include the start of the Second Sino-Japanese War on 7 July 1937, [REF:
assertions.subject: Dates

assertions.claim_text: The German Empire was dissolved in the German revolution of 1918–1919, and a democratic government, later known as the Weimar Republic, was created.
assertions.subject: The German Empire

assertions.claim_text: The interwar period saw strife between supporters of the new republic and hardline opponents on both the political right and left.
assertions.subject: The interwar period

assertions.claim_text: Italy, as an Entente ally, had made some post-war territorial gains; however, Italian nationalists were angered that the promises made by the United Kingdom and France to secure Italian entrance into the war were not fulfilled in the peace settlement.
assertions.subject: Italian nationalists

assertions.claim_text: From 1922 to 1925, the fascist movement led by Benito Mussolini seized power in Italy with a nationalist, totalitarian, and class collaborationist agenda that abolished representative democracy, repressed socialist, left-wing, and liberal forces, and pursued an aggressive expansionist foreign policy aimed at making Italy a world power, promising the creation of a "New Roman Empire".
assertions.subject: the fascist movement

assertions.claim_text: Following Hindenburg's death in 1934, Hitler proclaimed himself Führer of Germany and abolished democracy, espousing a radical, racially motivated revision of the world order, and soon began a massive rearmament campaign.
assertions.subject: Hitler

assertions.claim_text: France, seeking to secure its alliance with Italy, allowed Italy a free hand in Ethiopia, which Italy desired as a colonial possession.
assertions.subject: France

assertions.claim_text: The situation was aggravated in early 1935 when the Territory of the Saar Basin was legally reunited with Germany, and Hitler repudiated the Treaty of Versailles, accelerated his rearmament programme, and introduced conscription.
assertions.subject: The situation

assertions.claim_text: The United Kingdom, France and Italy formed the Stresa Front in April 1935 in order to contain Germany, a key step towards military globalisation; however, that June, the United Kingdom made an independent naval agreement with Germany, easing prior restrictions.
assertions.subject: the United Kingdom

assertions.claim_text: The Soviet Union, concerned by Germany's goals of capturing vast areas of Eastern Europe, drafted a treaty of mutual assistance with France.
assertions.subject: The Soviet Union

assertions.claim_text: Before taking effect, though, the Franco-Soviet pact was required to go through the bureaucracy of the League of Nations, which rendered it essentially toothless.
assertions.subject: the Franco-Soviet pact

assertions.claim_text: In the mid-to-late 1930s, Japanese forces in Manchukuo had sporadic border clashes with the Soviet Union and Mongolia.
assertions.subject: Japanese forces

assertions.claim_text: On 23 August, the Soviet Union signed a non-aggression pact with Germany, after tripartite negotiations for a military alliance between France, the United Kingdom, and Soviet Union had stalled.
assertions.subject: the Soviet Union

assertions.claim_text: In early June 1940, the Italian Regia Aeronautica attacked and besieged Malta, a British possession.
assertions.subject: the Italian Regia Aeronautica

assertions.claim_text: To assist Italy and prevent Britain from gaining a foothold, Germany prepared to invade the Balkans, which would threaten Romanian oil fields and strike against British dominance of the Mediterranean.
assertions.subject: Germany

assertions.claim_text: On 22 June 1941, Germany, supported by Italy and Romania, invaded the Soviet Union in Operation Barbarossa, with Germany accusing the Soviets of plotting against them; they were joined shortly by Finland and Hungary.
assertions.subject: they
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: The Germans also launched a North African offensive in January, pushing the British back to positions at the Gazala line by early February, followed by a temporary lull in combat which Germany used to prepare for their upcoming offensives.
assertions.subject: The Germans

assertions.claim_text: By 16 January 1945, this offensive had been repulsed with no strategic objectives fulfilled.
assertions.subject: this offensive

assertions.claim_text:
, where the Allied forces prosecuted prominent members of the political, military, judicial, and economic leadership of Nazi Germany for crimes against humanity]]

The Allies established occupation administrations in Austria and Germany, both initially divided between western and eastern occupation zones controlled by the Western Allies and the Soviet Union, respectively.
assertions.subject: The Allies

assertions.claim_text: Germany lost a quarter of its pre-war (1937) territory.
assertions.subject: Germany

assertions.claim_text: Italy lost its monarchy, colonial empire, and some European territories.
assertions.subject: Italy

launched from a fixed site in Peenemünde, 21 June 1943]]

Aircraft were used for reconnaissance, as fighters, bombers, and ground-support, and each role developed considerably.
assertions.subject: Aircraft
assertions.source_section: Advances in technology and its application

assertions.claim_text: Innovations included airlift (the capability to quickly move limited high-priority supplies, equipment, and personnel); and strategic bombing (the bombing of enemy industrial and population centres to destroy the enemy's ability to wage war).
assertions.subject: Innovations
assertions.source_section: Advances in technology and its application

assertions.claim_text: In the wake of Europe's devastation, the influence of its great powers waned, triggering the decolonisation of Africa and of Asia.
assertions.subject: the influence

assertions.claim_text: The Nazis killed an estimated 2.8 million ethnic Poles in addition to Polish-Jewish victims of the Holocaust.
assertions.subject: The Nazis

assertions.claim_text:

On 22 June, the Soviets launched a strategic offensive in Belarus that nearly destroyed the German Army Group Centre.:
assertions.subject: the Soviets

assertions.claim_text: The Soviet Union and the US emerged as rival superpowers, setting the stage for the Cold War.
assertions.subject: The Soviet Union

assertions.claim_text: Millions died in genocides, including the Holocaust, and by massacres, starvation, and disease.
assertions.subject: Millions

assertions.claim_text: On 6 and 9 August, the US dropped atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki in Japan.
assertions.subject: the US

(Saint Petersburg), 10 December 1942]]

By October, Axis powers had achieved operational objectives in Ukraine and the Baltic region, with only the sieges of Leningrad and Sevastopol continuing.
assertions.subject: (Saint Petersburg
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: The German campaign for air superiority started in August but its failure to defeat RAF Fighter Command forced the indefinite postponement of the proposed German invasion of Britain.
assertions.subject: The German campaign

assertions.claim_text: The causes of World War II included unresolved tensions in the aftermath of World War I, and the rise of fascism in Europe and militarism in Japan.
assertions.subject: The causes

assertions.claim_text: World War II is generally considered to have begun on 1 September 1939, when Nazi Germany, under Adolf Hitler, invaded Poland, after which the United Kingdom and France declared war on Germany.
assertions.subject: World War II

assertions.claim_text: Key events preceding the war included Japan's invasion of Manchuria in 1931, the Spanish Civil War, the outbreak of the Second Sino-Japanese War in 1937, and Germany's annexations of Austria and the Sudetenland.
assertions.subject: Key events

assertions.claim_text: Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war.
assertions.subject: Tanks

assertions.claim_text: In December 1941, Japan attacked American and British territories in Asia and the Pacific, including Pearl Harbor in Hawaii, leading the United States to enter the war against the Axis.
assertions.subject: Japan

assertions.claim_text: World War II is the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of over 60 million people.
assertions.subject: World War II

assertions.claim_text: Poland was also invaded by the Soviet Union in mid-September, and was partitioned between Germany and the Soviet Union under the Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact.
assertions.subject: Poland

assertions.claim_text: By mid-1941, Yugoslavia and Greece had also been defeated by Axis countries.
assertions.subject: Yugoslavia

assertions.claim_text: In early 1943, Axis forces were defeated in North Africa and at Stalingrad in the Soviet Union.
assertions.subject: Axis forces

assertions.claim_text: An Allied invasion of Italy in July resulted in the fall of its fascist regime, and Allied offensives in the Pacific and the Soviet Union forced the Axis to retreat on all fronts.
assertions.subject: An Allied invasion

assertions.claim_text: Many countries whose industries had been damaged moved towards economic recovery and expansion.
assertions.subject: Many countries

assertions.claim_text:
at a German Nazi political rally in Nuremberg, August 1933]]
Adolf Hitler, after an unsuccessful attempt to overthrow the German government in 1923, eventually became the chancellor of Germany in 1933 when President Paul von Hindenburg and the Reichstag appointed him.

The Kuomintang party in China launched a unification campaign against regional warlords and nominally unified China in the mid-1920s, but was soon embroiled in a civil war against its former Chinese Communist Party (CCP) allies and new regional warlords.
assertions.subject: The Kuomintang party

assertions.claim_text: photograph reenacting the removal of the Polish border crossing in Sopot
Although by 1942 resistance groups formed in most occupied territories, [REF:
In the 1930s, Britain and the United States together controlled almost 75% of world mineral output—essential for projecting military power.
assertions.subject: Britain

assertions.claim_text: In 1940, the Soviet Union annexed the Baltic states and parts of Finland and Romania, while Germany conquered Norway, Denmark, Belgium, Luxembourg, and the Netherlands.
assertions.subject: the Soviet Union

assertions.claim_text: After the Allied victory, Germany, Austria, Japan, and Korea were occupied, and German and Japanese leaders were tried for war crimes.
assertions.subject: the Allied victory

assertions.claim_text: Most historians agree that World War II began with the German invasion of Poland on 1 September 1939 and the British and French declarations of war on Germany two days later.
assertions.subject: Most historians

assertions.claim_text: Nearly all of the world's countries participated.
assertions.subject: Nearly all

assertions.claim_text: In 1944, the Western Allies invaded France at Normandy, and the Soviet Union advanced into Central Europe.
assertions.subject: the Western Allies

assertions.claim_text: During the same period, Japan suffered major setbacks, including the crippling of its navy by the United States, the loss of key Western Pacific islands, and defeats in South-Central China and Burma.
assertions.subject: Japan

assertions.claim_text:
battle the Imperial Japanese Army forces in Taierzhuang, 1938]]

In July 1937, Japan captured the former Chinese imperial capital of Peking after instigating the Marco Polo Bridge incident, which culminated in the Japanese campaign to invade all of China following years of tension and low-level conflicts.
assertions.subject: Japan

assertions.claim_text: Nazi policy entailed that Wehrmacht subject Soviet POWs to murderous treatment, executing all Jewish and Communist POWs immediately per the Commissar Order, and subjecting the remainder to forced marches to open-air concentration camps, where they were to be deliberately starved to death.
assertions.subject: Nazi policy
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: By the end of the winter of 1941, 2.8 million Soviet POWs had died in German captivity.
assertions.subject: 2.8 million Soviet POWs
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: Some 3.3 million Soviet POWs would die in German captivity by the war's end in total, a nearly 60% mortality rate.
assertions.subject: Some 3.3 million Soviet POWs
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: The diversion of three-quarters of the Axis troops and the majority of their air forces from France and the central Mediterranean to the Eastern Front prompted the United Kingdom to reconsider its grand strategy.
assertions.subject: The diversion
assertions.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

assertions.claim_text: Japanese advances were accompanied by numerous atrocities, including the Sook Ching Massacre in Singapore.
assertions.subject: Japanese advances

assertions.claim_text: Following the capture of Bataan, Japanese armies forced some 75,000 Filipino and American prisoners on a 42km death march, resulting in thousands of deaths.
assertions.subject: Japanese armies

assertions.claim_text: Despite considerable losses, in early 1942 Germany and its allies stopped a major Soviet offensive in central and southern Russia, keeping most territorial gains they had achieved during the previous year.
assertions.subject: early 1942 Germany

assertions.claim_text: The fortress city of Sevastopol, which the Red Army had held out against Axis siege for nearly 250 days, was finally seized with the use of massive artillery bombardments and poison gas.
assertions.subject: The fortress city

assertions.claim_text: An estimated 60 million to more than 75 million people died in the war including at least 20 million who died from deprivation, famine and disease.
assertions.subject: An estimated 60 million to more than 75 million people

assertions.claim_text: Civilian deaths have been estimated to comprise 67% to 80% of all direct and indirect deaths from the war.
assertions.subject: Civilian deaths

assertions.claim_text: The Soviet Union had the highest overall death toll (estimated at 20 million to 28 million), [REF:
assertions.subject: The Soviet Union

assertions.claim_text:
during the Nanjing Massacre in December 1937]]
In trials following the war, representatives of the Axis powers and their accomplices were convicted of numerous war crimes, crimes against humanity and complicity in genocides.
assertions.subject: representatives
assertions.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

assertions.claim_text: The Nazis killed about 6 million Jews in a racially motivated genocide known as the Holocaust.
assertions.subject: The Nazis
assertions.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

assertions.claim_text: They also killed millions of Slavs, over 130 thousand Romani, and members of other groups that they considered racially inferior.
assertions.subject: They
assertions.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

assertions.claim_text: Almost 300,000 people with mental and physical disabilities were also systematically killed in Germany and its occupied territories.
assertions.subject: Almost 300,000 people
assertions.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

assertions.claim_text: The Russian Academy of Sciences reported in 1995 that civilian victims in the Soviet Union at German hands totalled 13.7 million dead, twenty percent of the 68 million people in the occupied Soviet Union.]
assertions.subject: The Russian Academy
